mappings.rationale: Provides foundational chemistry concepts -- chemical bonds, reaction kinetics, and molecular structure -- that underpin the organic and inorganic chemistry of living systems (A, B).
Thermodynamics and reaction energetics are essential for understanding metabolism, enzyme kinetics (B.7), and drug action mechanisms (C.2).
Covers organic chemicals in life processes (B) including carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, DNA, and biochemical reactions central to this section.
Directly covers enzymes (B.7), their role in chemical reactions, and their relationship to proteins and DNA in vital processes.
Directly addresses hormones (B.9) including vertebrate hormones (B.9.b), endocrine-neural regulation (B.9.a), and reproductive hormones.
Covers the molecular structures of carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids (B.1-B.5) and metabolism at the chemical level.
Covers the chemistry of carbon-based molecules including alkaloids, steroids, and terpenes (B.10) and the organic basis of drugs (C).
Addresses DNA and RNA structure and function (B.5.b and B.5.c), nucleic acids central to vital processes.
Directly covers drug classification (C.4), drug action (C.2), absorption and metabolism of drugs (C.3), and drug addiction (C.7).
Covers vitamins (B.8), proteins (B.3), and the metabolic role of nutrients in vital processes.
Directly relevant to drug use and abuse and the nature of drug addiction and dependence (C.7).
Relevant to drugs affecting the immune response system (C.4.h) and drugs directed against disease organisms (C.5).
Covers antibiotics (C.5.a), chemotherapeutic drugs (C.5.b), and drugs directed against disease organisms (C.5).
Directly relevant to drugs directed at the suppression of cancer (C.6), including drug development and treatment.
Covers antibiotics and antibiotic resistance (C.5.a), and microbial toxins (E.3) relevant to biocides and biotoxins.
Covers drugs affecting the central and autonomic nervous systems (C.4.c and C.4.d), opioids, and drug action mechanisms (C.2).
Relevant to the evolution of hormones (B.9.a) and the relationship between endocrine and neural regulation of metabolism.
Covers nucleic acids (B.5), DNA and RNA, and the molecular biology of proteins (B.3) central to vital processes.
